title: Reflection: DictTable/DictField walk with labels resolved once, DictEnum value2Label, guarded dynamic call
instruction: In the sandbox model, create a class DemoReflectionUtil exercising the runtime metadata API. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="reflection-dict"). Public static methods: (1) `void dumpRecord(Common _record)` - constructs `new DictTable(_record.TableId)` ONCE outside any loop, iterates fields with fieldCnt()/fieldCnt2Id(i)/fieldObject(fieldId), skips system fields, and infologs `dictField.label()` plus the value read dynamically as `_record.(fieldId)`; re-constructing the DictTable per field FAILS the case. (2) `str enumLabel(EnumId _enumId, int _value)` - returns `new DictEnum(_enumId).value2Label(_value)`. (3) `void runIfPresent(ClassId _classId, str _methodName)` - constructs a DictClass and calls callStatic() only after hasStaticMethod() returned true; an unguarded dynamic call FAILS the case. Every Dict* object must be seeded from an intrinsic (tableNum/classNum/enumNum) or from a passed id, never from a string literal name. The class must build with zero BP errors.
